title: "Dell VxRail Physical Disk Predictive Failure and Unhealthy Drive Status"
criticality: "critical"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-19.1.47 · Dell VxRail Physical Disk Predictive Failure and Unhealthy Drive Status

## Description

This use case ingests **physical disk** health from a **Dell VxRail** environment using **three compatible planes**: **(1) VxRail Manager REST** JSON (recommended) where the manager aggregates per-node **physical disk** inventory and health bits such as **predictive_failure**, **disk_state**, **reallocated** sector style counters, **slot**, and **serial_number**; **(2) iDRAC9** text streams (**SEL** / **LCL**) from syslog, where Dell surfaces predictive-failure, offline, and SMART trip language in human-readable lines; and **(3) iDRAC9 Redfish** **Storage** / **Drives** JSON, which exposes `SerialNumber`, `Status` health/state, slot, and media type in a **schema** distinct from the VxRail Manager v1/v2 field spellings. The SPL defensively **coalesce()**s v1, v2, RedFish, and **iDRAC** variants (`predictive_failure` vs `PredictiveFailureCount` vs boolean strings), lowercases and matches **disk_state** across **FAILED/failed/Offline/unknown** families, and builds a **4-tier** `severity_bucket` (critical / warning / watch / ok) with **cache-tier** promotion so a **cache**-tier predictive pages harder than a **capacity**-tier one. A **per-(serial, src_kind) dedup** after **sort** handles repeated polls; a **stats** on `(cluster, node, serial)` then merges **REST** and **iDRAC** so the same physical **serial** does not double-ticket, while `values(src_kind)` still proves both planes saw the issue. The narrative boundary: this JSON is **not** the **vSAN** object / resync / component health detector (**UC-19.1.48**), and not **Dell Secure Remote Services** tunnel health (**UC-19.1.49**) — it is the **platter / NVMe / SAS** “is this **drive** sick” story in front of a **vSAN** rebuild. Pair with object-layer monitoring after you confirm a drive signal.

## Value

A **predictive** failure (PFA family, SMART trip, reallocated/pending growth) is the window in which a storage team opens a **Dell** support case, orders a **fru**, and **swaps** during **daytime** with change control, instead of a **3am** sev-1 when a **double-fault** is one bad hour away. A **failed** or **offline** **disk** under load directly drives **vSAN** **resync** and **rebuild** traffic, stealing **IOPS** from workloads and **shrinking** fault domains until the cluster returns to a healthy **decluster**; that rebuild window is exactly when a **second** device fault becomes an **unrecoverable** object story. **Cache-tier** loss hurts **write** and **absorption** of bursts more symmetrically than a single **capacity**-tier **SAS** drive in a large **HDD** set — business owners feel **latency** and **replication** backlog first, even before a full data-loss scenario. Catching the **PFA** early converts capital and logistics into a clean **RMA** path; catching only the **catastrophic** failure converts the same day into a capacity + performance + **risk** call with everyone **online**.

## Implementation

Create `index=vxrail`. Deploy **Option A** (VxRail Manager → HEC, `sourcetype=vxrail:disks` at 900s) on a **Heavy Forwarder** and/or **Option B** (iDRAC9 → **SC4S** with `dell:idrac:lcl`) for **SEL** agreement. **Install** the **Dell** **PowerEdge** add-on (5263) to normalize iDRAC fields. Paste the `spl` from this file into a **saved** search, **schedule** `*/15` over **-2h**, **alert** when `severity_bucket` is in **(critical, warning, watch)** and **throttle 6h** on **serial** + **cluster**. Add the LCM and HCL or EOL lookups from **detailedImplementation** when the base alert is too chatty during change windows or EOL plan holds.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• A Splunk index that holds all streams used below (this document uses `index=vxrail` — create it or change every SPL and input consistently).
• VxRail Manager (VxRail Manager) reachable over HTTPS from the host that will run the scripted input or a collector container; account with System Administrator-class rights on the VxRail side or a read-only custom role you validate against `/rest/vxm/v1` and `/rest/vxm/v2` in a lab. Treat credentials as high-value: rotate and store in a secrets manager, not in a git repo.
• Dell iDRAC9 (Lifecycle Controller) on each VxRail node, syslog permitted to your Splunk Connect for Syslog (SC4S) tier or a Heavy Forwarder `UDP/TCP:514` listener; **RedFish** and WS-Man enabled per Dell security hardening, not exposed to the public Internet.
• Network: outbound 443/tcp to VxRail Manager; iDRAC management VLAN to SC4S; HEC to indexers on 8088 (or your standard) if you use the Python HEC poller in Option A. Clock sync (NTP) on the collection host and on VxRail nodes so `**serial_number**` correlation lines up across API and SEL in the same wall-clock window.
• Scope boundary: this is UC-19.1.47 (physical drive / SMART / VxRail Manager disk API). Sibling UC-19.1.48 covers vSAN object, component, and resync health at the storage-object layer, not the same events. Sibling UC-19.1.49 is Dell Secure Remote Services (SRS) / tunnel health to Dell support — again not drive telemetry. If your symptom is "object unhealthy" or "resync stalled," start there after you know the underlying drive is or is not suspect.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

Option A (recommended) — VxRail Manager REST → HEC, sourcetype `vxrail:disks`

No Splunkbase TA emits a single canonical `vxrail:physical_disk` stream today; treat the sourcetype name as customer-defined and keep it stable across your estate. The VxRail Manager REST surface for physical disks is the `/rest/vxm/v2/system/disks` family on HTTPS to your management FQDN (some 7.x builds expose equivalent v1 paths — read your release’s API catalogue). Each poll should emit one JSON object per drive (or one JSON event containing an array, then split in props — the script below flattens to one HEC line per drive so field extraction is trivial).

1) On a Linux Heavy Forwarder (or a hardened utility host that can reach HEC and VxRail Manager), create a read-only credentials file with mode 0600 and owner the Splunk or automation user:

Path: `/etc/splunk-vxrail-creds.cnf`
```
VXRM_FQDN=vxm-prod.corp.example
VXRM_USER=splunk_vxrail_ro
VXRM_PASS=(vault-managed secret)
HEC_URL=https://idx01.corp:8088/services/collector/event
HEC_TOKEN=(HEC token with index=vxrail)
# Optional: set VXRM_VERIFY_CA=/opt/splunk/certs/enterprise-ca.pem
```

2) App layout: `TA-VxRail-Disks-HEC` under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/` with this `local/inputs.conf` binding a 15-minute `script://` (900 seconds) — drive health and API rate limits both favour quarter-hour collection in production, not 60s polling.

`$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-VxRail-Disks-HEC/local/inputs.conf`
```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-VxRail-Disks-HEC/bin/vxrail_disks_to_hec.py]
interval = 900
sourcetype = vxrail:disks
source = vxrail_manager:disks
index = vxrail
disabled = 0
```

3) `props.conf` for large JSON and indexed extractions (tune `TRUNCATE` upward if a multi-node array exceeds 20k characters):

`local/props.conf`
```ini
[vxrail:disks]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
TRUNCATE = 20000
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TIMESTAMP_FIELDS = _time
# If your HEC poller sets a JSON field named time in epoch seconds, map it in transforms or the poller so _time in the event matches receipt:
# TIME_PREFIX = "time"
# If you rely on receipt time, omit and Splunk will use _indextime for rough ordering only — prefer a JSON epoch from the poller.
```

4) `bin/vxrail_disks_to_hec.py` (illustrative — add retries, `requests.Session`, and pagination if your VxRail returns `nextPage` or per-node sub-queries). The script: load creds, GET `https://<vxm>/rest/vxm/v2/system/disks` with HTTP Basic (local VxRail Manager user) *or* swap in a vCenter-issued SSO bearer if your org uses the documented SSO path (replace the `auth=` tuple with `headers={"VMware-Api-Session-Id": sso_token}` after you implement token exchange — out of scope for a minimal paste, but the field names you flatten stay the same). For large clusters, you may also call `/rest/vxm/v2/system/nodes` first and then fan out per-node serial; merge into each disk record as `enclosing_node` so downstream SPL can resolve the host.

```python
#!/opt/splunk/bin/python3
import os, json, time, http.client
from urllib.parse import urlparse
import urllib.request

def read_cnf(path):
    d = {}
    with open(path) as f:
        for line in f:
            line=line.strip()
            if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line: continue
            k,v=line.split("=",1)
            d[k.strip()]=v.strip()
    return d

def hec_post(url, token, payload):
    o=urlparse(url)
    conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection(o.hostname, o.port or 443, timeout=120)
    body = json.dumps({"event": payload, "sourcetype": "vxrail:disks", "source": "vxrail_manager:disks", "time": int(time.time())})
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Splunk {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    conn.request("POST", o.path, body, headers)
    r = conn.getresponse()
    _ = r.read()
    if r.status not in (200, 201):
        raise SystemExit(f"HEC {r.status}")

def main():
    cnf = read_cnf("/etc/splunk-vxrail-creds.cnf")
    vxm = f"https://{cnf['VXRM_FQDN']}/rest/vxm/v2/system/disks"
    handler = None
    if os.environ.get("VXRM_INSECURE") == "1":
        import ssl
        handler = urllib.request.HTTPSHandler(context=ssl._create_unverified_context())
    o = urllib.request.build_opener(handler) if handler else urllib.request.build_opener()
    req = urllib.request.Request(vxm)
    import base64
    b64 = base64.b64encode(f"{cnf['VXRM_USER']}:{cnf['VXRM_PASS']}".encode()).decode()
    req.add_header("Authorization", f"Basic {b64}")
    req.add_header("Accept", "application/json")
    with o.open(req, timeout=120) as resp:
        raw = json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
    # Adjust to the JSON envelope your VxRail returns — common patterns: { "data": [ {...}, ... ] } or plain list
    data = raw.get("data", raw) if isinstance(raw, dict) else raw
    if isinstance(data, dict) and "data" in data:
        data = data["data"]
    for disk in (data if isinstance(data, list) else []):
        # Promote a stable cluster_id field for SPL — replace with a field your API returns
        if isinstance(disk, dict):
            disk.setdefault("ingest_tier", "vxm_disks")
            hec_post(cnf["HEC_URL"], cnf["HEC_TOKEN"], disk)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

5) Cross-source correlation — the serial_number on the VxRail JSON *must* match the drive serials shown in iDRAC/SEL. When you add Option B, search both streams with a common `serial` in SPL; the Step 2 pipeline deduplicates on `(serial, src_kind)` so a duplicate REST row does not page twice, while `values(src_kind)` still lists both `vxm` and `sel` for audit.

Option B — iDRAC9 SEL / Lifecycle Controller Log via syslog (Splunk + SC4S)

1) In iDRAC Settings → iDRAC Settings → Syslog), send OS Log, Lifecycle Controller (LCL), and System Event Log (SEL) to your SC4S VIP on UDP/514 (pilot) or TCP/514/6514 (preferred for loss-sensitive sites).

2) On SC4S, add a `props`-style match or a unique port program to set `sourcetype=dell:idrac:lcl` (org naming varies: `dell:idrac:sel` is also common — include both in the UC SPL with OR, or map to one with `nullQueue` aliasing in transforms).

Example receiver stanza on a Heavy Forwarder if you are not on SC4S yet (lab only; production should use the SC4S container pattern in your `splunk_config` directory):

`local/inputs.conf`
```ini
[udp://514]
connection_host = ip
sourcetype = dell:idrac:lcl
index = vxrail
no_appending_timestamp = false
```

3) `props.conf` for iDRAC text lines — vendor strings differ; a resilient pattern extracts slot and serial from common Dell substrings. Install Splunk Add-on for Dell EMC PowerEdge ([Splunkbase 5263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5263)) and align your sourcetype with the TA’s OOTB extractions; if a field name differs, add FIELDALIAS- in the same app.

`local/props.conf`
```ini
[dell:idrac:lcl]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
# Example extractions; validate against _raw in dev:
EXTRACT-ids_slot = [Dd]isk\s+(\d+|\S+\s*slot[: ]\s*(\d+))
EXTRACT-ids_ser  = [Ss]erial(?:\s+number)?[:\s]+([A-Z0-9-]{6,32})
EXTRACT-ids_msg  = (?i)Predictive\W*[Ff]ailure|Offline|PFA|SMART|re-?allocated|uncorrect
```

4) The PowerEdge TA (5263) does not replace VxRail Manager — it is the underlay (iDRAC) hardware plane. VxRail-specific lifecycle (LCM) events may still be clearer in the VxRail Manager API, so run Option A and B together, not A alone, when compliance asks for a second opinion.

Option C — iDRAC9 Redfish / JSON poller, sourcetype `dell:idrac:redfish`

1) For each VxRail node, poll `https://<iDRAC>/redfish/v1/Systems/System.Embedded.1/Storage` with BASIC iDRAC credentials (read-only) or Redfish session tokens, follow Members to each `Storage/Controllers` then Drives subordinate URLs as Redfish tree requires (some iDRAC builds nest drives under an array). Emit one HEC line per drive with a flattened object that includes: `SerialNumber`, `Status.Health`, `Status.State`, `Oem.Dell` predictive flags when present, `SlotNumber`, and `MediaType`. Use the same HEC target as Option A, different `sourcetype`.

2) `local/inputs.conf` with `script://.../idrac_redfish_drives_to_hec.py` at 900s to align with VxRail Manager. Keep serial_number consistently named; the main SPL in Step 2 already expects `SerialNumber` via `coalesce()`.

3) If RedFish `Storage` returns an empty `Members` list while the iDRAC UI shows drives, your iDRAC license or security profile has blocked that namespace: confirm iDRAC9 Enterprise (Dell) for full Storage inventory on modern builds.

4) Reiterate serial correlation — Redfish, VxRail Manager, and SEL may all speak about the *same* physical disk; the UC’s dedup and values(src_kind) keep one logical row in the final table and show evidence both planes saw the condition.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

Paste the SPL below in Search & Reporting, confirm results in a non-production window, then Save as a report, clone to an alert, and schedule. The pipeline: (1) `src_kind` tags each event by sourcetype so a drive seen through REST and iDRAC remains distinguishable for deduplication while still merging on serial in `stats`. (2) `pnum` / `pstr` and `snum` / `sstr` absorb v1 vs v2 and boolean-vs-string field shapes for predictive and SMART-like indicators. (3) `state` is lowercased to collapse FAILED/Failed and to align vSAN/ESA OSA string variants. (4) `dedup` on `(serial, src_kind)` followed by a `stats` on `(cluster, node, serial)` is the dedup and cross-source join: one combined row with `sources=vxm+sel` when both report the same serial. (5) `severity_bucket` is a 4-tier triage: critical (failed / offline family or cache predictive), warning (predictive or SMART, or very high reallocated), watch (non-zero reallocated or pending with no trip yet), ok (hidden by the `where` before alert). (6) `eventstats` adds cluster-level failed and predictive counts to show blast radius. Remove the `where severity_bucket...` line for a dashboard that must show `ok` rows.

Schedule cron `*/15 * * * *` with a search window of last 2 hours — disk state changes on minute boundaries are rare; 15m aligns the poll interval. In **Settings → Searches, reports, and alerts** set throttle / alert suppression on serial (and optionally `cluster`) to 6 hours so a sticky predictive that has not been replaced does not re-page every 15m.

```spl
index=vxrail (sourcetype="vxrail:disks" OR sourcetype="dell:idrac:redfish" OR sourcetype="dell:idrac:lcl" OR sourcetype="dell:idrac:syslog") earliest=-2h
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype=="vxrail:disks", "vxm", sourcetype=="dell:idrac:redfish", "redfish", match(sourcetype, "idrac:lcl|idrac:syslog")>0, "sel", 1=1, "other")
| eval pnum=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(PredictiveFailureCount, **predictive_failure**, "0")))
| eval pstr=lower(tostring(coalesce(predictive_failure, PredictiveFailure, pf_state, "")))
| eval pred=if(pnum>0 OR match(pstr, "(?i)true|1|yes|pfa|predict|prefail"), 1, 0)
| eval state=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(disk_state, DiskState, Drive_State, status, Status, health, Health, ""))))
| eval snum=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(smart_alert, SmartAlert, smart_status, "0")))
| eval sstr=lower(tostring(coalesce(smart_alert, SmartAlert, smart_status, "")))
| eval smart=if(snum>0 OR match(sstr, "(?i)true|1|yes|warn|trip"), 1, 0)
| eval realloc=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(reallocated_sectors, ReallocatedSectorsCount, reallocated_sector_count, "0")))
| eval pending=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(pending_sectors, PendingSectorsCount, "0")))
| eval slot=coalesce(slot, Slot, drive_slot, DriveSlot, "")
| eval serial=coalesce(tostring(serial_number), tostring(SerialNumber), tostring(drive_serial), tostring(driveSerialNumber), "")
| eval cluster=coalesce(cluster_id, ClusterId, vxrail_cluster, cluster_name, "default")
| eval node=coalesce(host, ServiceTag, idrac_host, system_host, system_id, "unknown")
| eval media=lower(tostring(coalesce(media_type, MediaType, drive_type, form_factor, "")))
| eval dr=lower(tostring(coalesce(disk_role, DiskRole, drive_role, device_role, "")))
| eval is_cache=if(match(dr, "(?i)cache|tier-?1|writeback|cache_tier|performance"), 1, 0)
| where serial!="" AND len(serial)>=6
| sort 0 - _time
| dedup serial, src_kind
| stats latest(state) as state, latest(pred) as pred, latest(smart) as smart, max(realloc) as realloc, max(pending) as pending, latest(slot) as slot, latest(media) as media, latest(is_cache) as is_cache, values(src_kind) as sources, max(_time) as _time by cluster, node, serial
| eval is_failed=if(state IN ("failed", "offline", "critical", "error", "unhealthy", "degraded", "bad", "down") OR match(state, "(?i)fail|crit|unhealthy|degraded|error|offline|bad"), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    is_failed=1, "critical",
    pred=1 AND is_cache=1, "critical",
    pred=1 OR smart=1, "warning",
    realloc>=50 OR pending>=10, "warning",
    realloc>0 OR pending>0, "watch",
    1=1, "ok")
| eventstats sum(eval(if(is_failed=1,1,0))) as failed_in_cluster, sum(eval(if(pred=1,1,0))) as predict_in_cluster by cluster
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical",4, severity_bucket="warning",3, severity_bucket="watch",2, 1=1,0)
| where severity_bucket IN ("critical","warning","watch")
| sort 0 - sev_order - pred - realloc
| table _time, cluster, node, serial, slot, media, is_cache, state, pred, smart, realloc, pending, severity_bucket, failed_in_cluster, predict_in_cluster, sources
```

**Step 3 — Validate**

1) Inventory sanity (disk count): `index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:disks" earliest=-1h | eval serial=coalesce(tostring(serial_number),tostring(SerialNumber), "") | where serial!="" | stats dc(serial) as drive_count by cluster` — the count should be close to nodes × drives per node in your as-built (for example, 3 nodes with 8 front bays ≈ 24 visible drives, plus boot devices depending on what the API includes — document your baseline, then alert on a sudden drop, not a small delta).

2) Cross-source state consistency:
```
index=vxrail (sourcetype="vxrail:disks" OR sourcetype="dell:idrac:lcl") earliest=-1h
| eval serial=coalesce(tostring(serial_number),tostring(SerialNumber),"")
| eval state=lower(tostring(coalesce(disk_state,DiskState,status,Status,"")))
| stats values(state) as states by serial
| where mvcount(states)>1
```
Investigate if one plane says failed and another still healthy — often an RMA ghost in API inventory or a lagging SEL.

3) SMART trend (reallocated sectors) (swap field names to match your feed):
`index=vxrail (sourcetype="vxrail:disks" OR sourcetype="dell:idrac:redfish") earliest=-30d
| eval serial=coalesce(tostring(serial_number), tostring(SerialNumber), "")
| timechart span=1d max(reallocated_sectors) as max_realloc by serial` — a slow monotonic max is aging media; a step-change on many serials the same day is a firmware or environmental event.

4) Predictive count over time by cluster:
`index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:disks" earliest=-30d
| eval pnum=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(PredictiveFailureCount, predictive_failure, "0")))
| eval pstr=lower(tostring(coalesce(predictive_failure, PredictiveFailure, pf_state, "")))
| eval pred=if(pnum>0 OR match(pstr, "(?i)true|1|yes|pfa|predict|prefail"), 1, 0)
| timechart span=1d sum(pred) as predict_count by cluster` — if sum of predictives per day jumps for many clusters, think bad batch, power, or cooling, not a single sloppy slot.

5) Slot vs serial:
`index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:disks" earliest=-7d
| eval serial=coalesce(tostring(serial_number), tostring(SerialNumber), "")
| eval slot=coalesce(tostring(slot), tostring(Slot), "")
| stats values(slot) as slots by serial | where mvcount(slots)>1` — expect movement after re-seat or RMA; a surprise move is a data-quality warning.

6) Cluster health bridge (sibling UC-19.1.48 preview):
`index=vxrail (sourcetype="vxrail:disks" OR sourcetype="vxrail:vsan" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:*")
| eval cluster=coalesce(cluster_id, ClusterId, "default")
| eval ds=lower(tostring(coalesce(disk_state, DiskState, "")))
| eval vh=coalesce(vsan_object_health, component_health, "")
| stats values(ds) as disk_states, values(vh) as vSAN_layer_hint by cluster`
This is a soft check — the exact vSAN sourcetype in your org may differ. When you stand up UC-19.1.48, make this join your standard “drive → object resync” story. UC-19.1.47 is authoritative for PFA/physical; do not conflate a vSAN object alert here without a drive signal unless your process says so.

**Step 4 — Tune for your environment**

1) Per-media thresholds — add `lookups/vxrail_drive_thresholds.csv` with `media_type,vendor,model,max_reallocated_sectors,max_pending_sectors` and apply after `stats` with `| lookup vxrail_drive_thresholds media AS media OUTPUT ... | eval severity_bucket=if(realloc>max_reallocated...`. SSDs, spinning SAS, and NVMe have different “normal” background realloc rates.

2) Cache vs capacity — the `is_cache` heuristic keys off VxRail / Redfish disk_role text; in vSAN OSA you expect explicit cache vs capacity; in **vSAN ESA** the strings move toward storage tier naming — use ESA-aware `match()` when your lab shows real strings. Policy: a cache-tier predictive is paged harsher than a capacity-tier predictive, because write performance and rebuild asymmetry is worse at the cache.

3) LCM window suppression — `lookups/vxrail_lcm_windows.csv` with `cluster_id,change_id,start_epoch,end_epoch` and `| where _time<start OR _time>end OR isnull(lcm_tag)` in a child macro so rolling updates do not fire predictive noise during iDRAC reset.

4) HCL / false vendor warnings — if VxRail UI shows a drive out of HCL or catalog mismatch, you may see spurious health flags. Cross-link your CMDB to Cisco UC-19.1.21-style HCL story only as a metaphor (that UC is Intersight); operationally, run Dell’s VxRail compatibility lists for the authoritative answer.

5) Multi-cluster fleet: build a timechart of `dc(serial)` for predict=1 per `cluster` and a second panel `sum` over all clusters for morning check dashboards—management cares about a 20% step-up across the fleet more than a single drive in one rack.

6) ESA vs OSA: keep both disk_state and disk_group in mind — ESA all-NVMe may label tiers differently. Extend the `is_cache` `match` when your VxRail JSON includes explicit vSAN tier strings.

7) `vxrail_planned_eol.csv` — some SSDs at end-of-life have a standing PFA=1 with Dell-blessed replacement in 30 days; add those serials to a suppression table until the physical swap, not a blind mute of the index.

8) HEC batch sizing — if a single HEC post exceeds the HTTP limit, split the drive array in the Python HEC poller; 20k `TRUNCATE` is there to protect the indexer, not to encourage megabyte lines.

9) vCenter integration — the SPL in this file does not query vCenter; if you have vSAN health in VMware TA data, the join belongs in a child search or a Data Model; keep this saved search on physical evidence first to avoid a pure-object mis-fire.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

(1) REST 401/403 with “known good” user — VxRail Manager local vs SSO vs vCenter identity paths differ; verify which realm your `VXRM_USER` is in and that lockout is not tripped. Test with `curl` from the poller host using the same Basic header.

(2) API shows predictive, UI is green — often the drive is in RMA status but still electrically present (“ghosted” inventory) — confirm with iDRAC SEL and a physical ticket.

(3) Double rows for one drive — you enabled Option A and B without dedup in an ad-hoc search; use the main UC SPL or a `| dedup serial, src_kind` before manual stats.

(4) vSAN rebuild started but you never had PFA — some failures are sudden (no SMART lead-in). This UC is PFA+health-biased; the sudden story is the vSAN object/health UC (19.1.48) plus iDRAC “hard” SEL events.

(5) reallocated frozen at zero — the OS of the ESXi host may not pass raw SMART; you need iDRAC (Option B/C) or VxRail Manager (A).

(6) SAS capacity PFA stuck without failure — EOL and planned replacement — see EOL CSV in Step 4; do not re-page weekly.

(7) 429 / connection reset from VxRail — raise interval to 1800s, reduce parallel per-node test scripts, and confirm NTP-aligned `If-Modified-Since` is not in play on your poller (some labs add amateur caches).

(8) iDRAC syslog late by >600s — UDP drops on a busy mgmt — switch the SC4S path to TCP or RELP.

(9) Unknown ESA `disk_state` string — the vSAN 8+ ESA world adds labels your 7.x OSA `case` never saw; extend `is_failed` with one lab capture of the raw `state` token.

(10) Duplicate alerts at LCM — firmware reload may reset SMART counters on swap; key serial not slot; slots move on reseat.

(11) Redfish `Storage: []` empty — iDRAC license or LC policy blocked Storage enumeration — validate Enterprise and permit the namespace.

(12) HEC 400 with “invalid token” — the token is bound to a HEC input that cannot write your index — fix outputs and index ACL on the HEC, not the Python.

(13) Cross-index false join — a Nutanix or NFS host in the same `vxrail` index (mis-tagging) will show nonsense node — enforce sourcetype filters in inputs and ACLs.

## SPL

```spl
index=vxrail (sourcetype="vxrail:disks" OR sourcetype="dell:idrac:redfish" OR sourcetype="dell:idrac:lcl" OR sourcetype="dell:idrac:syslog") earliest=-2h
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype=="vxrail:disks", "vxm", sourcetype=="dell:idrac:redfish", "redfish", match(sourcetype, "idrac:lcl|idrac:syslog")>0, "sel", 1=1, "other")
| eval pnum=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(PredictiveFailureCount, predictive_failure, "0")))
| eval pstr=lower(tostring(coalesce(predictive_failure, PredictiveFailure, pf_state, "")))
| eval pred=if(pnum>0 OR match(pstr, "(?i)true|1|yes|pfa|predict|prefail"), 1, 0)
| eval state=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(disk_state, DiskState, Drive_State, status, Status, health, Health, ""))))
| eval snum=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(smart_alert, SmartAlert, smart_status, "0")))
| eval sstr=lower(tostring(coalesce(smart_alert, SmartAlert, smart_status, "")))
| eval smart=if(snum>0 OR match(sstr, "(?i)true|1|yes|warn|trip"), 1, 0)
| eval realloc=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(reallocated_sectors, ReallocatedSectorsCount, reallocated_sector_count, "0")))
| eval pending=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(pending_sectors, PendingSectorsCount, "0")))
| eval slot=coalesce(slot, Slot, drive_slot, DriveSlot, "")
| eval serial=coalesce(tostring(serial_number), tostring(SerialNumber), tostring(drive_serial), tostring(driveSerialNumber), "")
| eval cluster=coalesce(cluster_id, ClusterId, vxrail_cluster, cluster_name, "default")
| eval node=coalesce(host, ServiceTag, idrac_host, system_host, system_id, "unknown")
| eval media=lower(tostring(coalesce(media_type, MediaType, drive_type, form_factor, "")))
| eval dr=lower(tostring(coalesce(disk_role, DiskRole, drive_role, device_role, "")))
| eval is_cache=if(match(dr, "(?i)cache|tier-?1|writeback|cache_tier|performance"), 1, 0)
| where serial!="" AND len(serial)>=6
| sort 0 - _time
| dedup serial, src_kind
| stats latest(state) as state, latest(pred) as pred, latest(smart) as smart, max(realloc) as realloc, max(pending) as pending, latest(slot) as slot, latest(media) as media, latest(is_cache) as is_cache, values(src_kind) as sources, max(_time) as _time by cluster, node, serial
| eval is_failed=if(state IN ("failed", "offline", "critical", "error", "unhealthy", "degraded", "bad", "down") OR match(state, "(?i)fail|crit|unhealthy|degraded|error|offline|bad"), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    is_failed=1, "critical",
    pred=1 AND is_cache=1, "critical",
    pred=1 OR smart=1, "warning",
    realloc>=50 OR pending>=10, "warning",
    realloc>0 OR pending>0, "watch",
    1=1, "ok")
| eventstats sum(eval(if(is_failed=1,1,0))) as failed_in_cluster, sum(eval(if(pred=1,1,0))) as predict_in_cluster by cluster
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical",4, severity_bucket="warning",3, severity_bucket="watch",2, 1=1,0)
| where severity_bucket IN ("critical","warning","watch")
| sort 0 - sev_order - pred - realloc
| table _time, cluster, node, serial, slot, media, is_cache, state, pred, smart, realloc, pending, severity_bucket, failed_in_cluster, predict_in_cluster, sources
```

## Visualization

**Table** of `cluster, node, serial, slot, severity_bucket, pred, smart, realloc, failed_in_cluster` (sort: critical first). **Single value**: count of `serial` where `severity_bucket`=`critical` in the window. **Timechart** of **predict=1** count by `cluster` (7d) for **fleet** regressions. **Drill** to a **raw** search for one **serial** across **(vxrail:disks, dell:idrac:lcl, dell:idrac:redfish)**. Optional **Gantt**-style LCM overlay by joining `vxrail_lcm_windows` for executive change correlation only — keep the main alert on physical evidence.
